data.body:
G4 container child-consistency audit (parent f89d-e54d-203d-4ee0 × THIS child 33d0 "Collapse the dual outbound applier paths to one production path"), all 7 cross-child interaction modes checked against the WHOLE sibling roster (2359/59ef/822a/8937/fe57) under the high-confidence / benign-reading filter, repo-corroborated against the AS-SHIPPED code. VERDICT: clean pairing — NO G4 cross-child finding; findings list EMPTY.

This audit supersedes the recurring prior G4 finding on this ticket (the 33d0↔2359 "labels_applied list-vs-int representation conflict between 33d0's surviving applier and 2359's count-canary"). That finding is WITHDRAWN as resolved by the as-shipped 33d0 collapse + 2359 integer-count design. Repo re-verification below.

REPO RE-VERIFICATION (current HEAD, post-collapse + post-2359):
- 33d0 shipped: `_apply_outbound_update` (apply_outbound.py:125-161) is now a THIN DELEGATOR — it converts the Mutation via `_mutation_to_batch_dict` and calls `update_one`, mirroring `_apply_outbound_delete`. The ~240-line parallel implementation (and its list-valued `labels_applied` payload key) is GONE; a repo search for `labels_applied` in apply_outbound.py returns only a docstring mention (L139), no list assignment. The leaf stays as a DISTINCT dispatch binding (typed_dispatch.py:56, reconcile.py:75) because the typed registry requires one callable per valid (direction×action) — so the dispatch-coverage invariant is preserved, not stranded.
- 2359 shipped (depends_on 33d0): per-sub-op counts are minted FRESH in the surviving batch path as INTEGERS — batch_dispatch.py:496 (`_labels_computed = _labels_applied = 0`), :515 (`_labels_applied += 1`), :593 (`"labels_applied": _labels_applied`). applier.py:803-805 surfaces `outcome["labels_applied"]/["comments_applied"]/["links_applied"]` uniformly as ints (default 0). The canary predicate (applier.py:814-819) `_subop.get(f"{kind}_computed",0) > 0 and _subop.get(f"{kind}_applied",0) == 0` over kinds (labels,comments,links) is WELL-TYPED over all three because every operand is an int — the `[] != 0` / truthiness hazard the prior finding posited is structurally impossible in the shipped design.

7-MODE WALK (parent × 33d0):
- Implicit shared state — BENIGN. 33d0 co-edits batch_dispatch.py/applier.py with 2359, but `2359 depends_on 33d0` (confirmed in 2359 deps) sequences collapse-then-instrument; 33d0 lands the single survivor first, then 2359 threads integer counters into it. No unguarded concurrent co-edit.
- Conflicting assumptions — BENIGN. The labels representation conflict is gone (see re-verification): the surviving applier produces integer counts for all three kinds, and 2359's count-based canary assumes integers — mutually consistent. 2359's cited "parity with inbound links_applied" (an int) is now an accurate model for every sub-op kind.
- Dependency gap — BENIGN. 33d0 is pure outbound-code consolidation needing no schema/fixture input, so (unlike 822a/59ef which correctly declare depends_on fe57) its lack of an input dep is appropriate. The 33d0→2359 sequencing edge is correctly carried on 2359's side.
- Scope overlap — BENIGN. 33d0 AC3 (outbound-only combined multi-sub-op UPDATE asserting each sub-op took effect = consolidation correctness), 2359 (computed>0 && applied==0 canary), 822a AC1 (bidirectional inbound↔outbound round-trip), 8937 AC3 (snapshot-entry key/shape contract) are distinct intents over the same production path. 33d0 declares telemetry OUT (→story E/2359) consistently.
- Ordering violation — BENIGN. The 33d0→2359 order is captured by `2359 depends_on 33d0`; both closed in that order. 33d0 also verifies behavioral parity BEFORE deleting the retired path (AC2 / test_outbound_update_combined.py parity gate), so the deletion cannot silently drop a capability.
- Consumer impact — BENIGN. Once 33d0 collapses to one path, 8937 AC3 / 822a AC1 (which drive "the production path") target the survivor by construction; neither edits the applier files, so they are not entangled in 33d0's rewrite. The collapse is additive-then-subtractive behind a parity check.
- Residual references — BENIGN. 33d0 deleted the dead typed-leaf parallel logic AND its now-misleading tests (test_applier_outbound_update.py, test_applier_outbound_links.py per file_impact). The leaf survives only as a thin delegator (dispatch-registry requirement), so dispatch bindings are not stranded. No sibling references the deleted parallel implementation — 2359 instruments the surviving batch path; 8937/822a/fe57/59ef key on the inbound snapshot/fetch surface or drive the production path, not the deleted leaf.

The roster-level parent anti-change-detector-guardrail criterion is a G3 coverage matter (owned by story B/8937 per the parent AC's explicit "(owned by story B)" clause) — not a G4 cross-child inconsistency, so not addressed here.

VERDICT for this pairing (parent × 33d0): no G4 cross-child finding; all 7 interaction modes read benign. The single recurring prior finding (labels list-vs-int) is withdrawn as resolved by the as-shipped collapse + integer-count design. Findings list is EMPTY.
